sample.task_prompt:
Using only MCP tools: create a 100x100 rectangle named Banner, then call node_resize to set it to exactly 320 wide by 90 tall. When you are done, call viewport_zoom_to_fit, then verify the result with the available MCP tools and report what you found.

At the very end of your final message, state whether the task went smoothly. If everything worked without problems, end with exactly "verdict: approve". If you hit any problems, bugs, or blockers, briefly say what went wrong and end with exactly "verdict: not-approve".

**Browser viewport**
width:1280
height:720

**Starting URL:** http://cua_designpen.web

Using only MCP tools: use create_shape to build a FRAME 400x300 named Canvas at (0,0), then a RECTANGLE 100x100 named Box at (20,20) parented into Canvas, and a TEXT node named Label at (20,140). When you are done, zoom the canvas to fit (press Shift+1 in the browser), then take a browser screenshot of the app so the result can be visually verified.
While completing this workflow, actively try to break the app through the tools: call create_shape with type "CIRCLE" (not a valid shape type); call create_shape with width -50 and then width 0; call create_shape with x set to the string "ten" instead of a number; call create_shape omitting the required width parameter entirely; and pass a 5000-character name. Confirm each invalid call returns a structured status/code/message error rather than silently creating a broken node. Recover from any errors and finish the workflow described above.
Log every defect you find using exactly this format, one block per defect:
DEFECT #<n> Steps: <exact MCP tool calls with their arguments to reproduce> Observed: <what actually happened> Expected: <what should have happened> Severity: <critical | major | minor | cosmetic>
Broken rendering, crashes, freezes, silent failures (invalid input accepted but nothing happens or a success response is returned), partial application of a batch that was supposed to be atomic, state lost after navigation or reload, inconsistent state between the canvas and the tool responses, and free-form or unstructured error messages (any error that is not a structured status/code/message payload) all count as defects. If you found no defects, state exactly "NO DEFECTS FOUND". At the very end of your final message, end with exactly "adversarial-report: complete".

**Browser viewport**
width:1280
height:720

**Starting URL:** http://cua_designpen.web
